title: lithocarpus aggregatus
intro: Lithocarpus aggregatus, also known as the Burmese walnut or mountain walnut, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region and parts of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds and bark, which have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The seeds of Lithocarpus aggregatus are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as reduced risk of chronic diseases and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Lithocarpus aggregatus, including decoctions and infusions, can be prepared from the seeds, bark, and leaves, and are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
